steps.title: Spot the timeline difference
steps.content: Text 1 claims the moon formed “over the course of thousands of years.” Text 2 claims it formed “immediately after impact, taking just a few hours.” This timeline contrast is the key signal about how the authors differ.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: Choice D correctly identifies the difference: Text 1 sees moon formation as a slow process; Text 2 sees it as fast. Choices A, B, and C don't target this—A confuses slow formation with earlier timing, while B and C introduce irrelevant comparisons about Theia and surface similarity. That matches choice D — D is correct.
